w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: VG Global Logistics — Product & Engineering Team

ZITO is a next-generation logistics super-app purpose-built for Kenya and East Africa by VG Global Logistics. It serves B2B, B2C, C2C, and C2B logistics needs through a unified digital platform connecting Customers, Drivers, Transporters, Agents, Agencies, and Admins in a single, intelligently managed ecosystem.

ZITO empowers Transporters to white-label and manage their own fleets; Agents to manage customer portfolios; Agencies to operate franchise-style businesses; and Admins to control assignment logic and platform-wide configuration with granular precision.
Customers, Drivers, Transporters, Agents, Agencies, Admins
Freight booking, fleet management, bidding marketplace, multi-role portals
Manual, Semi-Auto, and Full Auto modes (Admin-controlled)
To ensure consistency throughout this document and across all teams, the following terms are defined and used uniformly:
A freight request created by a Customer, Agent, or Transporter.
When referring to the record created in the system.
The physical execution of a Booking — from assignment to delivery.
When referring to driver activity, status updates, and trip charges.
A synonym for Booking used in the marketplace context — a load posted for bidding.
When referring to marketplace listings and bidding workflows.
Cargo or freight to be transported — used in marketplace and driver demand context.
When referring to available work in the marketplace.
A bid or price submission made by a driver, transporter, or agent on a Booking/Order.
When referring to the bidding and negotiation workflow.
Rule: In API endpoints and code, Booking is the canonical term. Trip and Order are used contextually in UI and communications.

The East African logistics market is fragmented, informal, and largely offline. Businesses struggle with no unified platform, manual coordination via WhatsApp, no real-time tracking, and opaque pricing.
Digital booking, live tracking, full history & receipts
Full white-label portal with fleet, driver & customer management
Manual booking for clients, no tracking
Agent portal with customer portfolio & booking management
Full control with analytics, compliance & assignment engine
ZITO is a multi-role platform. Each role has a dedicated portal, distinct capabilities, and RBAC-controlled access levels enforced at the API middleware layer.
Unrestricted access — configure, override, and manage the entire platform
Day-to-day booking and driver assignment management
All financial reporting, invoices, and payment reconciliation
Book, track, and review freight orders
Accept trips, update status, manage wallet & earnings
Manage own fleet, drivers, customers, and bookings
Manage customer portfolios and earn commission on bookings
Manage multiple agents/transporters in a franchise model
Highest authority — unrestricted access to all modules, data, and configuration.
Manage all users across all roles.
Control Assignment Engine mode (Manual / Semi-Auto / Full Auto).
Configure global settings, pricing, vehicle types, and surcharges.
Access all financial data, reports, and analytics including profit breakdown.
Override any order, assignment, or status in the system.
Use View As mode to impersonate any user role for support and QA.
View and manage all bookings and driver assignments.
Assign or reassign drivers to orders.
Cannot change pricing, system settings, or access financial data.
View all transactions, payment records, and profit reports.
Manage contract rates with Transporters and Agents.
Cannot manage operational assignments or user accounts.
Register and manage their account profile.
Create, track, and manage bookings with real-time price estimates.
Rate completed trips and provide feedback.
View full booking history, download PDF receipts/invoices.
Register with license, vehicle, and compliance document details.
Receive and respond to trip assignments within configured time windows.
Update trip status through the full lifecycle (Assigned → Delivered).
View wallet earnings, ratings, and trip history.
Submit expenses (toll, fuel etc.) during trips.
Manage own fleet of vehicles (add, edit, retire).
Invite, assign, and monitor own drivers.
Manage own customers and create bookings on their behalf.
Set custom pricing tiers for customers (within Admin-defined limits).
Access fleet KPI dashboard, financial summaries, invoices, and payment history.
All Transporter activity is visible to Admin.
Create bookings on behalf of assigned customers.
Track booking and delivery status across all customers.
Earn configurable commission per completed booking.
Create and manage Agent and Transporter sub-accounts.
View consolidated dashboard across all managed accounts.
Manage Agency-level contracts and pricing agreements.
Access Agency-level financial reports and export data.
The Assignment Engine is a core differentiator of the ZITO Platform. Admins have full control over how orders are assigned to drivers.
Admin reviews each order and manually selects a driver. No automation.
System suggests the best drivers based on rules. Admin confirms or overrides.
System auto-assigns and notifies driver. Admin can still override.
High-volume, standard orders with trusted drivers
Proximity — driver must be within a configurable radius of the pickup location.
Availability — driver must be available with no active or conflicting trip.
Vehicle Match — driver's vehicle type must match the booking's required vehicle type.
Rating Threshold — driver must meet or exceed the
Cargo Capacity — vehicle weight and volume capacity must meet cargo requirements.
Customer Preference — preferred or whitelisted drivers are prioritised.
Cost Optimisation — Admin can configure cost-first vs speed-first weighting.
Admin can switch Assignment Mode at any time from the Settings panel.
Specific order categories (e.g., VIP, hazardous cargo) can enforce fixed modes.
Admin can blacklist a driver from a specific customer's orders.
Admin can whitelist a driver for a specific customer (always prioritised).
Transporter Admins can manage assignment rules within their own fleet, subject to global constraints.
ZITO uses a multi-portal architecture. Each role has a dedicated portal with tailored UI and functionality.
Live KPI cards: Total Bookings, Active Bookings, Total Revenue, Pending Payments.
Pending approvals widget — drivers, transporters, agencies awaiting review.
View all orders with full filtering (status, role, date, vehicle type, region).
Manual or assisted driver assignment per order.
Bulk order actions: assign, cancel, export.
Order detail view with full timeline, status history, and audit log.
Create, edit, activate, and deactivate users across all roles.
Driver verification — approve documents, license class, and background check.
View all vehicles across all Transporters and independent drivers.
Vehicle document tracking — insurance, inspection, NTSA, with expiry alerts.
Assign vehicles to drivers; block expired or unverified vehicles automatically.
Impersonate any role: Customer, Driver, Transporter, Agent, or Agency.
Select a specific user to preview their exact portal experience.
Agents can create bookings on behalf of customers (offline orders).
Assist drivers with trip issues and handle negotiation between customer and drivers.
Update booking status manually if required.
users have controlled admin access with limited permissions.
Select vehicle type with visual cards showing capacity and indicative price range.
Enter pickup and delivery addresses with Google Maps autocomplete.
Specify cargo details: type, weight, description, special handling instructions.
Real-time price estimation before confirming booking.
Optional: scheduled booking, recurring booking, multi-stop, special instructions.
Live map showing driver location with real-time status updates and ETA.
Full booking history with status filter.
List of assigned trips with key details.
Accept or reject trips within configured time windows.
Status updates: Assigned → Accepted → Picked Up → In Transit → Delivered.
Submit trip expenses (toll, fuel, food, emergency costs).
Driver wallet showing earnings from completed trips (hire rate).
Trip-wise income breakdown — daily, weekly, monthly views.
Upload and manage license, medical, and compliance documents.
Add, edit, and retire vehicles with full document tracking and expiry alerts.
Live fleet status board showing current driver and vehicle assignments.
Invite and onboard drivers; view performance, ratings, and trip history.
Assign or reassign drivers to trips; manage availability.
Create bookings on behalf of customers; configure customer-specific pricing.
Manage all orders for the fleet using internal assignment engine.
Monitor active, pending, and completed orders.
Fleet revenue summaries, driver earnings management, customer invoicing.
Contract management with configurable rate cards.
Customer Rate vs Hire Rate visibility with profit tracking.
View and manage Agent's customer portfolio.
Add new customers on behalf of ZITO or linked Transporters.
Create bookings on behalf of customers; track active bookings and status.
Monthly earnings summary and commission invoice generation.
Create and manage Agent and Transporter sub-accounts.
View consolidated activity across all sub-accounts.
Sub-account financial breakdown, contract and pricing management.
Consolidated reports across all managed accounts; export to CSV and PDF.
ZITO provides a dedicated marketplace dashboard for drivers, transporters, and agents.
Available Loads — view all open and future loads; filter by location, route, vehicle type.
My Bids — view submitted bids and track bid status (pending / accepted / rejected).
Submit Offer — accept customer rate or submit a counter-offer.
Location Interest Panel — select preferred locations/routes and request loads before traveling.
Priority Loads — loads matching driver's selected route/location highlighted for faster action.
Notifications — new load alerts, bid updates, assignment confirmations.
Every booking on ZITO follows this defined lifecycle from creation to financial closure:
Booking request submitted. Awaiting Admin review.
Load broadcasted to marketplace participants (if bidding enabled).
Drivers / Transporters / Agents submitting offers and counter-offers.
Booking approved. Ready for driver assignment.
Driver and vehicle assigned. Driver notified.
Booking cancelled subject to cancellation policy.
Booking or assignment rejected with reason recorded.
Rule: A booking cannot be marked Completed without payment confirmation. Admin override is available in exceptional cases.
All bookings follow a defined status lifecycle with full audit logging at every transition.
Booking created and awaiting admin review or assignment.
Booking approved, ready for driver assignment.
Driver and vehicle assigned to the booking.
Driver has accepted the trip assignment.
Cargo delivered; Proof of Delivery (POD) uploaded.
Delivery and payment confirmed; booking closed.
Booking cancelled subject to cancellation rules.
Booking or assignment rejected with reason recorded.
After assignment — Cancellation charge may apply.
After trip start — Full or partial charge applicable.
May lead to restriction or penalty.
Admin can override penalties with mandatory reason recording.
System tracks cancellation reason, time, and penalty applied.
Pickup ETA and Delivery ETA provided to customer in real time.
System tracks expected vs actual times and delay deviations.
Delay alerts sent to Admin and Customer automatically.
Used for performance tracking and service quality improvement.
ZITO uses a rules-based pricing engine. All prices are calculated in real-time and shown to the customer before booking confirmation. No hidden fees.
Predefined pricing for regular customers based on contractual agreements.
Fixed rates for specific routes, vehicles, or customers.
Calculated based on distance, vehicle type, and load type.
Pricing determined through bidding and bargaining between customer and service providers.
ZITO follows a structured two-rate system per trip:
Amount charged to the customer for the trip.
Amount paid to the transporter/driver for executing the trip.
ZITO earns the difference: Customer Rate - Hire Rate.
ZITO allows dynamic trip-level charges to be added during or after trip execution:
Road toll charges incurred during the trip.
Fuel adjustments required during long or unexpected routes.
Charges for loading cargo at pickup location.
Charges for unloading cargo at delivery location.
Waiting time charges beyond the free window.
Driver expenses (food, emergency costs) — submitted by driver, approved by Admin.

Drivers may incur expenses during trip execution such as fuel, toll, food, and emergency costs. These expenses are recorded under trip charges, can be submitted by drivers (optional), reviewed and approved by Admin, and included in trip-level financial calculations.
Each trip follows a clear financial model:
Sum of all approved trip charges
Customer Rate - Hire Rate - Trip Expenses
ZITO enforces strict financial visibility to protect platform margins:
Total price only — no breakdown of hire rate or platform margin.
Their earnings (hire rate) only — not customer rate or platform profit.
Own revenue and hire rate — not platform profit margin.
Full breakdown: customer rate, hire rate, expenses, and profit.
Full control — add, edit, delete any charge.
Can submit expenses (optional, subject to Admin approval).
Reports available per trip, driver, transporter, and time period:
Customer Rate, Hire Rate, Total Expenses, and Profit per trip.
ZITO operates a multi-party logistics marketplace where customers, drivers, transporters, and agents interact through a bidding and negotiation system.
Step 1: Customer submits a booking request with offer price (optional), load details, and vehicle requirements.
Step 2: System broadcasts the load to relevant participants based on vehicle type, location, availability, and role.
Step 3: Participants can accept the offered price, submit a counter-offer (bid), or negotiate via admin-controlled workflow.
Step 4: Admin can view all bids, compare rates, select best participant, and negotiate further.
Step 5: Final rate and selected driver/transporter can be shared with customer for approval.
Step 6: Once approved, booking is confirmed, assigned to selected driver/transporter, and converted to active trip.
Admin/system can post open or future loads.
Drivers, transporters, and agents can browse, bid, and reserve available loads.
Ensures better vehicle utilization, faster load matching, and increased platform activity.
ZITO provides a proactive load request system where drivers and transporters can express interest in specific locations or routes before moving.
Drivers select preferred location/destination and indicate intent to move toward it.
System prioritizes participants who have shown interest in a route when new bookings are created.
Reduces empty travel (dead mileage) and improves driver trip planning.
Drivers with active location interest get early visibility of matching loads.
Drivers completing trips can request return loads.
System prioritizes drivers near destination who need return loads.
Reduces empty return trips and improves fleet efficiency.
After the first 15 minutes of waiting at pickup
Per additional stop beyond the primary delivery
Partial or full payment before trip begins.
Allowed for Admin-approved customers only — credit limit defined by Admin.
Cash payments are not supported initially to maintain financial control and platform integrity.
Customer creates booking; payment type selected.
Trip marked delivered; payment awaited if not prepaid.
Booking marked completed after payment confirmation.
Rule: Trip cannot be financially closed without payment confirmation. Admin override available in exceptional cases.
Credit available for approved customers — not limited to contract customers.
Outstanding balance tracking with overdue alerts.
Booking restriction automatically applied if credit limit is exceeded.
System tracks advance paid, pending amount, payment status (paid/unpaid/partial), and payment mode (
All payments are routed through the platform to ensure financial control, transparency, and margin protection.
ZITO maintains a wallet system for drivers providing full financial transparency:
Wallet includes: earnings from completed trips (hire rate), approved expenses, and pending payouts.
Drivers can track total earnings, view trip-wise income, and view any deductions.
Payments released after trip completion or settlement cycle — Admin-controlled payout approval.
Core data model for ZITO (PostgreSQL 16 via
ORM). All tables use UUID primary keys,
column naming, and soft delete (paranoid mode).
Email OTP for login verification (free tier)
Trip charges module (customer rate, hire rate, profit)
Email notifications for key booking events.
Google Maps integration: autocomplete, routing, and live ETA.
Live driver tracking (visible to customers and admins).
Full Auto Assignment Engine with ML-based driver scoring.
Advanced analytics and Business Intelligence dashboards.
Full Bidding Marketplace with negotiation workflows.
Proof of Delivery with mandatory photo upload.
Enterprise API access for B2B customer integrations.
Offline & Low Connectivity Support — drivers update trip status offline, data syncs when network is available.
JWT tokens with configurable expiry (default 30 days).
Mandatory email OTP 2FA enforced at every login.
RBAC enforced at API middleware level — no UI-only restrictions.
All API endpoints require authenticated JWT, except /register and /login.
All secrets stored as environment variables — never hardcoded.
HTTPS enforced in production (Railway handles SSL termination).
Database access restricted to application server IP in production.
Admin audit log for all sensitive actions (user changes, order overrides).
Configurable data residency: Railway EU/US regions.
Activity tracking for suspicious actions — flags accounts automatically.
Admin can restrict or suspend users flagged for suspicious activity.
Blacklist system prevents flagged users from interacting with the platform.
/v1/. Authenticated endpoints require a valid JWT Bearer token.
Send OTP to email or mobile
Lock or unlock user data fields
List or add customers to portfolio
Create booking on behalf of customer
View all bookings across managed accounts
Add a charge to a trip
Get all charges for a trip
Trip financial summary (customer rate, hire rate, expenses, profit)
ZITO enforces strict verification across all user roles to prevent theft and fraud, ensure authenticity, meet regulatory requirements (KRA, NTSA, Kenya Police), and maintain operational safety.
User submitted documents; awaiting Admin review.
Admin verified all documents; user can operate.
Admin rejected application; user must re-apply.
Admin requested corrections; specific documents must be resubmitted.
No cargo tampering or unauthorised delivery under any circumstances.
No unjustified route deviation without Admin approval.
Real-time GPS tracking must be active during all trips.
Mandatory status updates at each stage of the journey.
Proof of Delivery photo required before marking booking as Delivered.
Permanent ban + legal escalation to authorities
Financial penalty; repeated violations lead to ban
All approvals on the ZITO Platform are controlled exclusively by ZITO Admin. No other role has the authority to approve documents, grant access, or override compliance decisions.
Step 1: User registers and creates their account.
Step 2: User uploads all required documents and compliance materials.
Step 3: Compliance status automatically set to pending.
Step 4: ZITO Admin reviews submitted documents and details.
Step 5: Admin sets final status — approved, rejected, or
After approval, critical identity and compliance fields are locked. Any change triggers
Responsible for cargo execution, safe handling, and delivery per SOP.
Responsible for the conduct and compliance of all drivers and vehicles registered under their account.
Responsible for platform operations, compliance framework, assignment integrity, and dispute resolution.
All payments must flow through the ZITO Platform — off-platform direct deals are prohibited.
Only ZITO controls assignment logic — Transporters and Agencies cannot override core rules.
Expired documents trigger automatic blocking — fresh Admin approval required for reactivation.
VG Global Logistics acts as the final authority for all disputes.
At any time via Admin portal
Rating window: 48 hours after Completed status. After that, the window closes.
Ratings are anonymous to the rated party.
Driver average rating recalculated after every submitted rating.
Drivers below Admin-configured minimum are flagged and may be suspended.
Damaged goods, missing items, wrong delivery location
Rude conduct, route deviation, no-show, abandonment
Unsafe vehicle, breakdown, wrong vehicle type sent
App error, booking not found, tracking not working
Any issue not covered by the above categories
Complaint filed with category, description, and booking reference.
Admin requested more information from a party.
Admin made a final decision and notified all parties.
Complaint closed after resolution or no response within timeout.
Opens in-app messaging or calls driver directly
Flags booking as delayed; Admin notified
Admin alerted; driver receives push notification
Admin notified; can update booking details in real time
I want to cancel this trip
Redirects to cancellation flow with policy displayed
Escalates to Admin immediately with booking + GPS data
Opens free-text support ticket linked to the booking
Opens in-app messaging or calls customer directly
Triggers waiting time policy; Admin notified after threshold
Flags trip as disrupted; Admin notified for reassignment
Opens cargo incident report form linked to booking
Escalates to Admin immediately with GPS location
Opens free-text support ticket linked to the trip
Instant Admin alert sent via dashboard notification, email, and SMS.
Driver GPS location captured and pinned at the moment of SOS trigger.
Booking frozen — no further status changes allowed until Admin intervenes.
Admin can call driver or customer directly from the SOS alert panel.
All SOS events permanently logged in the booking audit trail.
Free cancellation at any time before Admin approves
Driver has already committed and is travelling
Only Admin can cancel at this stage
Only Admin can cancel at this stage
Waiting time charges begin after the first 15 minutes (50 KES per 15 minutes — configurable).
After configurable maximum wait time (default: 30 minutes), driver can trigger No-Show.
Admin notified; Admin decides whether to cancel, extend, or reassign.
Critical alerts when user may not have app open
ZITO supports document handling for operational and compliance needs.
All data access enforced at API level using role-based scope filtering. No user can access data outside their defined scope regardless of the endpoint or request parameters.
Data Filter Applied at API Level
on all fleet, driver, customer, booking queries
on all customer and booking queries
on all agent, transporter, and booking queries
on all trip and document queries
on all booking, invoice, and receipt queries
No filter applied — full platform visibility
Role of user who created the booking
Linked transporter if handled by Transporter; NULL if handled by Admin
Admin can query deleted records using
Users, drivers, vehicles, bookings, documents, contracts, agents, agencies
Driver compliance and document records are NEVER deleted — even soft delete not permitted
All list API endpoints return paginated results. Default page size: 20. Maximum: 100.
Notification delivery runs as background jobs — never blocking the HTTP response.
Document expiry checks run as a daily scheduled job — flags and notifies 30 days before expiry.
All sensitive actions captured in an immutable audit log. Logs are written at the time of the action and cannot be modified or deleted by any user including Admin.
Login, logout, profile update, password reset, 2FA verification
Created, approved, assigned, status changes, cancelled, completed
Driver assigned, driver reassigned, vehicle swapped, assignment blocked
Approved, rejected, resubmission requested, blacklisted, data locked/unlocked
Payment held, released, refunded, frozen, dispute opened/closed
Any status override, View As session start/end, emergency freeze/disable
Bid submitted, bid accepted, bid rejected, load posted
Rate limit violations, failed auth attempts, cross-tenant access attempts
The logistics platform brand — developed and operated by VG Global Logistics.
A fleet owner or logistics company with multiple vehicles and drivers on the platform.
An individual who sources and manages customers, earning commission per completed booking.
An organisation managing multiple Agents or Transporters, enabling franchise-style operations.
The system that matches orders to drivers — operates in Manual, Semi-Auto, or Full Auto mode.
The amount charged to the customer for a trip.
The amount paid to the driver/transporter for executing a trip.
Additional charges added to a trip (toll, fuel, loading, waiting etc.).
Customer Rate - Hire Rate - Trip Expenses.
Gross Merchandise Value — total value of all bookings processed through the platform.
One-Time Password — 6-digit code sent to email/mobile for 2FA login verification.
Proof of Delivery — photo or document confirming cargo was delivered successfully.
Role-Based Access Control — each user role has defined permissions enforced by the API.
Admin feature to preview the platform from the perspective of any user role.
Return load optimization — drivers request loads for their return journey.
Empty travel with no load — minimized through the Location Interest System.
Know Your Customer — identity and document verification process.
National Transport and Safety Authority — Kenya's transport regulatory body.
Kenya Revenue Authority — Kenya's tax collection agency; KRA PIN required for drivers.
prompt sent to customer's phone to authorise payment.
ZITO Admin holds full override authority across all platform operations. This is a core design principle that ensures platform integrity, financial control, and operational safety.
Admin can override any fare, apply discounts, waive charges, and set custom rates for any customer or trip.
Admin can assign, reassign, or override any driver-booking assignment at any time regardless of the assignment engine mode.
Admin can release, freeze, refund, or hold any payment. Admin can waive cancellation fees or override payment status.
Admin is the final authority for all disputes. Admin decision is binding on all platform participants.
Admin can approve, reject, lock, unlock, or reset compliance status for any user or vehicle.
Admin can update any booking status at any lifecycle stage, including force-completing or force-cancelling trips.
Admin can remove bids, cancel offers, close auctions, and intervene in any negotiation workflow.
Admin can activate, deactivate, blacklist, or restore any user account instantly.
This centralised control model ensures that ZITO maintains full accountability, financial transparency, and operational authority over all platform activity at all times.
If booking has SOS freeze marker, status updates are blocked until unfreeze.
Frontend removed cash options in frontend/
Replaced pending placeholders with working responses:
now returns generated invoice-style data from completed bookings
now returns booking status and financial totals
Wired Help page to backend instead of placeholder alert:
Added booking ID input and live API submissions for help + SOS.
Backend app load smoke test passes (require(
Lint diagnostics on edited files: no errors.
Started and reviewed your codebase against the PRD.
Backend exists, but agency reports and export are still placeholders ("coming in Phase 2" in backend/
No dedicated Agency portal route/page in frontend App (no /portal/agency).
getFinanceSummary and getInvoices in backend/src/controllers/transporter.controller.js are still "coming in Phase 2".
triggers alert(...) with note that backend endpoints are pending.
Customer invoice endpoint is still marked as Phase 2 in backend/
Current payment invoice generation is HTML response, not full PDF pipeline per PRD.
Vehicle document upload is Phase 2 placeholder in backend/
User photo upload + notification preferences are Phase 2 placeholders in backend/
OTP persistence is not production-grade yet
Backend bidding offers exist, but no clear dedicated marketplace dashboard UI flow in frontend routes/pages.
Settings screen is mostly local mock state
has many toggles/values but mostly not wired to backend persistence.
If you want, next I can produce a
(Done / In Progress / Blocked) with exact API + UI tasks to close in priority order.
based on your PRD vs current implementation.
Build real endpoints for help actions + SOS escalation (not just UI alert).
Wire to notifications + audit trail + booking freeze
PRD says no cash initially; remove/disable cash option from frontend and enforce in backend validation.
Move OTP storage from in-memory to DB-backed table (
At least ship minimum useful data for operations.
P1 (Should finish in Phase 1, immediately after P0)
Replace HTML invoice fallback with real PDF generation and download endpoints.
Complete user photo and notification preferences endpoints.
Add basic marketplace page(s): available loads, my bids, submit offer, bid status.
Add /portal/agency with core dashboard + managed accounts + reports view.
P2 (Can trail after launch if needed)
Connect settings UI to backend system settings APIs.
Save/load real config instead of local-only state.
Filters, exports, and finance-grade aggregates across admin/agency/transporter.
Better assignment explainability, exception handling, and conflict indicators.
